results.editorial:
My Joyful Home Care Centre is a 20-bed nursing home located at 12 Jalan SS 19/3 in the established SS 19 neighbourhood of Subang Jaya. It positions itself as a boutique facility focused on delivering personalised elderly care, accepting residents across a range of dependency levels — including those requiring tube feeding, suctioning (including tracheostomy care), catheter management, and residents in a coma. The facility offers single, twin-sharing, and triple-bedded room options, suggesting it targets a range of budget points within a modest-sized setting.

My Joyful Home Care has a dedicated dementia programme, covering residents from mild through to severe stages, which is a meaningful commitment for a facility of this size. The website indicates 24-hour caregiver presence with trained, dedicated staff. Clinical capabilities listed publicly include complex wound care, airway management, and enteral feeding — coverage that goes beyond basic custodial care and positions this home to receive post-hospital discharge patients who still need active nursing input.

At just 20 beds, wait times for placement may apply and visiting during the selection process is advisable to assess staffing ratios firsthand. The facility has no published pricing — call to ask about monthly fees, what is included in the base rate, and whether there are additional charges for clinical procedures. Confirm current JKM licence status and ask whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight. The SS 19 location is convenient for families based in Subang Jaya and Petaling Jaya, with good road access from the Federal Highway.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Respite care, Post-hospitalisation
facts.red_flags: No published pricing; small capacity means availability may be limited

results.editorial:
Harvestars Care Centre at D'Island Puchong opened in April 2023 as the brand's flagship facility and is marketed as Malaysia's first nursing care home built in a stand-alone condo style. Located at 9 Jalan Barat 5 in the D'Island Residence township, the home occupies a triple-storey superlink building with a lift installed for resident transfers. The facility is listed as having approximately 100 beds and sits within an established residential area in Puchong, roughly 20 kilometres south-west of Kuala Lumpur.

Harvestars specialises in assisted living, post-hospitalisation recovery, dementia and Alzheimer's care, and palliative support. The care team includes registered nurses, caregivers, doctors, physiotherapists, and practitioners of traditional Chinese medicine, providing 24-hour coverage. Four meals per day are prepared by an experienced chef, with menus designed for special dietary needs. Resident amenities include karaoke, mahjong, massage rooms, and a cafeteria — a more activity-focused offering than is typical for this price bracket. The website lists nursing home fees starting from RM 4,899 per month and day care from RM 2,899 per month.

As a facility that opened in 2023, Harvestars D'Island is relatively new and its track record is still accumulating. Families should ask about nurse-to-resident ratios on night shifts, the extent of the TCM practitioners' role (supplementary wellness or clinical?), and the precise clinical capabilities available in-house versus those requiring hospital transfer. The condo-style layout may affect ventilation and outdoor space compared with landed equivalents — a site visit during the day is worthwhile. Verify the current JKM registration and enquire about whether the listed starting price covers all nursing needs or applies only to lower-dependency residents.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Dementia care, Palliative care, Post-hospitalisation, Day care
facts.languages: Mandarin likely (TCM staff, Chinese community marketing)
facts.red_flags: Relatively new (2023 opening); pricing starts at RM4,899/mo — confirm inclusions; condo-style layout limits outdoor space

results.editorial:
Sam Ching Kong Old Folks Home is a non-profit welfare home operated by the Sam Ching Kong Temple (三清宮道院与慈善福利會), a Taoist temple and charitable welfare society located in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Hulu Langat. The address is listed as Hulu Langat Batu 18, Sungai Makau, 43100 Hulu Langat, Selangor — a semi-rural location approximately 30 kilometres from central Kuala Lumpur. The home operates under the temple's charitable mission, offering a subsidised or donation-supported residential option for elderly individuals in need.

The facility's Google listing describes a clean and peaceful environment with friendly caretakers, suggesting a community-run home with a focus on basic residential care and companionship rather than clinical nursing. The data sheet records 50 beds, which would make it a mid-sized community welfare home by Selangor standards. As a temple-run facility in the Taoist tradition, it is likely to cater primarily to Buddhist and Taoist Chinese-speaking elderly, though this should be confirmed directly. No details are publicly available about nursing staffing levels, medical protocols, or dementia-specific programming.

Given its non-profit, donation-supported model, Sam Ching Kong is likely a more affordable option than private nursing homes — but families should ask directly about fees, if any, and whether government subsidies are available for eligible residents. Confirm whether registered nurses are on staff or whether care is primarily provided by lay caregivers, and clarify what medical emergencies protocol looks like given the semi-rural location. The nearest major hospital is likely Hospital Kajang or Hulu Langat District Hospital — ask about typical transfer times and the facility's relationship with local healthcare services.

facts.red_flags: Sparse online presence; no clinical details available; semi-rural location; welfare/non-profit model means limited nursing infrastructure

results.editorial:
Noble Care Rawang serves as the headquarters for the Noble Care Malaysia network, one of the larger private nursing home chains operating in Peninsular Malaysia with over 15 branches. The Rawang facility is located at D-10 Jalan LKS-4, Lee Kim Sai, 48000 Rawang — a residential area in northern Selangor roughly 35 kilometres from Kuala Lumpur. Noble Care describes itself as having over 16 years of experience in elderly care and positions Rawang as its founding and anchor location.

The Rawang branch provides a broad clinical menu: dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and respite care, stroke and cancer support, physiotherapy, and skilled nursing procedures including Foley's catheter management, nasogastric tube care, tracheostomy care, colostomy bag changes, bedsore dressing, and post-operative recovery. This is a substantive clinical capability set that makes the home suitable for higher-dependency residents. Round-the-clock nursing cover is listed on the website, with qualified nurses and experienced caregivers providing personalised attention.

Noble Care does not publish pricing on its website, so families will need to call to get a current fee schedule. As with any chain operator, service consistency can vary between branches — the Rawang HQ is likely to have stronger oversight and staffing given its flagship status, but confirming bed availability and current nursing ratios at the time of enquiry is advisable. Ask specifically about the medical review schedule (how often a doctor visits), the palliative care pathway, and whether dementia residents are housed in a separate unit or integrated with the general population. The facility shares a phone number (+60 16-278 6993) with the Ampang branch, so confirm you are speaking with Rawang staff when making enquiries.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite care, Stroke and cancer care, Physiotherapy
facts.red_flags: No published pricing; shares contact number with Ampang branch — confirm branch when calling

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Orang Tua Cahaya Maju is a welfare-based elderly care home operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Cahaya Maju (Cahaya Maju Welfare Association) at 22 Jalan Melati 1, Taman Seri Melati, 68100 Batu Caves, Selangor. Incorporated in January 2019, the centre is classified on its registration as providing residential care and is co-listed as a shelter catering to elderly individuals aged 40 and above, including those from low-income or vulnerable backgrounds. It operates daily from approximately 08:45 to 19:00 and accepts both monetary and in-kind donations, reflecting its non-profit community welfare model.

The organisation's charitable mandate centres on providing shelter, food, basic medical access, and a degree of social support to seniors who might otherwise lack these necessities. Google Maps classifies the listing as a homeless shelter alongside an elderly care function, indicating the home serves a mixed population of older adults in need rather than functioning as a clinical nursing home. The facility's capacity and staffing levels are not published, and no nursing or medical programming details are available through public sources.

Families seeking placement for a dependent elderly relative with significant clinical needs should approach this centre with an understanding that it operates as a welfare home rather than a licensed nursing facility. Ask directly about what level of personal and nursing care is provided, whether any medically trained staff are on site, and how medical emergencies are handled. The Batu Caves location provides reasonable access to Selayang Hospital (approximately 8 kilometres). Confirm current JKM registration status before placing a resident here.

facts.red_flags: Dual-listed as homeless shelter; welfare/non-profit model; clinical capabilities unverified; no nursing details available

results.editorial:
Stella Maris Home PLT Care Centre is a small residential care facility located in Seksyen 6, Shah Alam, reachable on 03-79606070. The data sheet records a capacity of 11 beds, which places it in the very small, boutique tier of Selangor care homes — likely a converted residential property rather than a purpose-built facility. The PLT (Perkongsian Liabiliti Terhad, or limited liability partnership) business structure is common among small private care operators in Malaysia. No website or social media presence was found in public searches, suggesting this is a quietly operating local home without significant digital marketing.

With 11 beds and no published clinical profile, the most reasonable expectation is that Stella Maris Home provides basic residential and personal care — assistance with daily activities, meals, and companionship — rather than a full nursing or dementia-specialist programme. The Seksyen 6 Shah Alam address is a well-served residential area with reasonable proximity to Hospital Shah Alam and several private clinics. The modest scale may appeal to families who prefer a home-like setting with closer staff-to-resident ratios, provided the care level matches their relative's needs.

Because no online profile exists beyond the Google Maps listing, families should visit in person before committing to placement. Key questions to ask include: what nursing or caregiver qualifications the staff hold, whether a registered nurse is available at any point or if the home relies entirely on trained caregivers, what the monthly fee covers, and whether the home holds a current JKM licence. For residents with dementia, mobility challenges, or active medical needs, confirm that the physical layout and staffing model can safely accommodate these requirements.

facts.red_flags: No online presence; only 11 beds; care level and clinical capability entirely unverified; visit in person strongly recommended before placement

results.editorial:
Aliz Nursing Care is based at 12A Jalan Badminton 13/29, Tadisma Business Park, Seksyen 13, Shah Alam — a commercial shopoffice park in central Shah Alam. Unlike most facilities on this guide, Aliz presents primarily as a home-based nursing care provider rather than a residential nursing home: the website and public listings emphasise one-to-one nursing care delivered at the patient's own home, with the commercial address serving as the operational base. Google Maps categorises the service as aged care, career guidance, and home health care, consistent with a hybrid model that may include some centre-based care alongside outreach.

Aliz's stated service range covers aged and elderly care, post-surgery recovery, disability care, injury care, personal care assistance, and 24-hour in-home and hospital nursing. The company indicates it employs local registered nurses trained to assist with activities of daily living, framing one-to-one home-based care as superior to institutional placement for both patient outcomes and family peace of mind. The emphasis on home care means Aliz is likely a good fit for families managing a parent who wishes to remain at home but requires professional nursing input beyond what a domestic helper can provide.

Pricing is not published. Families should call to understand whether Aliz offers any centre-based residential placements or exclusively provides nursing staff for home deployment. Confirm the qualifications and registration of nurses supplied, the minimum engagement duration, and how continuity of care is managed if the assigned nurse is unavailable. For families in Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, or nearby Petaling Jaya, Aliz represents a potentially convenient option for post-hospitalisation home nursing or ongoing personal care management.

facts.care_types: Home health care, Aged care, Post-surgery care, Disability care
facts.red_flags: Primarily home-based model, not a residential nursing home; confirm whether any residential beds are available; no published pricing

results.editorial:
Woodrose Senior Residences at Taman Sri Ukay, Lorong Hillview 2D, Ampang, is the third and newest facility in the Woodrose network, described on the brand's website as the group's most premium offering. Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd was formed from the merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd, and operates two further homes in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam alongside this Ampang location. The Taman Sri Ukay site is approximately 20 minutes from central Kuala Lumpur by car, set in a quieter hill-residential neighbourhood with elegant furnishings noted in the facility's own descriptions.

Woodrose is distinguished by its Shariah-compliant operating framework, described as a rarity in the Malaysian elderly care landscape. A full-time Ustaz oversees Islamic standards across food sourcing, cleanliness, daily activities, and spiritual programming — making this a particularly coherent choice for Muslim-Malay families who want a religiously aligned environment for a parent or grandparent. Care programmes span residential, nursing, memory care (dementia and Alzheimer's), palliative, respite, and day care. Published pricing for the Shah Alam homes starts at RM 3,800 per month for independent living and RM 4,500 per month for assisted living — though rates for the Ampang site should be confirmed separately as a newer and premium-positioned property.

Because specific bed counts and room-type details for the Taman Sri Ukay home are not published, families should request a site visit to assess room size, outdoor access, and the staffing model. Ask about nurse-to-resident ratios at night, how medical emergencies are handled, and what clinical procedures can be managed in-house versus what requires hospital transfer. The Shariah-compliant positioning means this home will attract a specific clientele — demand may result in a waiting list, so enquire early if placement timing is important.

facts.care_types: Assisted living, Nursing home, Dementia/memory care, Palliative care, Respite care, Day care
facts.red_flags: Pricing for Ampang site unconfirmed; bed count not published; newer location with less track record than Shah Alam homes

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Sri Tanjung is an exclusively Muslim women's care home located at 2990-A Lorong Melati, Kampung Damai, 48050 Kuang, Rawang, Selangor. The centre is registered with the Department of Social Welfare (JKM) under registration number B/PJBWT006/2019 and has been in operation for over 30 years, giving it among the longer operational histories of any community-based elderly care home in northern Selangor. Its single-gender, single-faith mandate is clearly stated — the home accepts Muslim women only — making it a specific resource for families seeking a religiously congruent environment for elderly female relatives.

Sri Tanjung offers flexible care arrangements including monthly residential placement, temporary or transit stays, and daily packages running from 7am to 7pm. Social media content shows residents engaging in group activities, and the TikTok and Facebook presence describes comprehensive care services (perkhidmatan jagaan yang menyeluruh). The JKM registration confirms a basic standard of government oversight. The home appears to operate on a community welfare footing, accepting enquiries via phone at +60 18-209 8986 and directing potential residents through a standard intake process.

The care level offered at Sri Tanjung should be discussed directly, as the home's positioning as a welfare-oriented community centre means its clinical nursing capabilities may be limited compared to a private nursing home. Families should ask what nursing qualifications the staff hold, how medical needs above a basic threshold are managed, and whether the daily rate and monthly rate structures differ significantly. The Kuang location is relatively rural — confirm access to the nearest hospital (Hospital Selayang, approximately 20 kilometres) and how medical emergencies are handled. For a Muslim family seeking a culturally appropriate, women-only residential option with a long track record, this home is worth investigating.

facts.care_types: Nursing home (women-only), Respite care, Day care
facts.red_flags: Clinical nursing capability unverified; rural location; welfare-model footing; Muslim women only — not suitable for other demographics

results.editorial:
Noble Care Malaysia Ampang is one of the network's Klang Valley branches, situated at 537 Jalan 5, Taman Ampang Utama, 68000 Ampang, Selangor. Ampang Utama is a long-established residential neighbourhood with good access to hospitals — Ampang Hospital is approximately 5 kilometres away, and Gleneagles Hospital Kuala Lumpur is reachable within 15 minutes — making this a relatively well-serviced location for a nursing home. The Noble Care brand operates more than 15 branches nationally and has over 16 years in the sector, positioning itself as a professional, multi-location aged care provider.

The Ampang branch offers the group's standard clinical menu: 24-hour nursing supervision, dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative and respite support, physiotherapy, and skilled nursing procedures including Foley's catheter care, tracheostomy management, nasogastric tube care, colostomy bag changing, bedsore dressing, and post-operative care. The website also lists capability for cerebral palsy care and care for individuals with stroke and cancer diagnoses. The facility describes itself as providing a home-like environment with nutritious meals and social activities for residents.

Bed count and room-type details are not published for this branch, and no pricing is listed — families should contact Noble Care directly at +60 16-278 6993, noting that this number is shared with the Rawang HQ. Ask to speak specifically with the Ampang team to confirm current availability, local pricing, and staffing levels. As with all chain operators, quality can vary between branches, so a site visit to the Ampang location specifically is worthwhile rather than drawing conclusions from the network's overall reputation. Confirm whether dementia residents have a dedicated unit or are integrated into the general ward.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite care, Physiotherapy, Post-hospitalisation
facts.red_flags: Shared contact number with Rawang HQ — specify Ampang branch when calling; no published pricing or bed count
